# Alfredo

> Alfredo is the home for your projects: it deploys your next one in minutes with auth, email, database and analytics already wired, and watches every project from one HQ on your own servers.

Alfredo does two things. When you deploy a project, you pick the integrations it needs and Alfredo creates the keys and config each one needs and wires them in automatically. You never copy a secret into a .env file, first project included. Integrations already running for other projects are reused. After that, every project reports to one HQ: one dashboard for traffic, email delivery, errors, payments and uptime across all your projects, and it gets more useful with every project you add.

Alfredo runs on your own servers. Any VPS you can SSH into works; Alfredo does not host your projects. The code is open source. Alfredo is pre-launch today: this site collects waitlist emails and nothing else.

Integrations Alfredo starts with: Umami for analytics, Postmark for email, Convex for the database, Better-Auth for auth, Creem for payments, Sentry for errors, GitHub, and Uptime Kuma for uptime. The lineup grows from what you ask for.

## Pages

- [Alfredo](https://getalfredo.com/): the landing page, with the interactive HQ demo and the waitlist
- [Privacy](https://getalfredo.com/privacy): what happens with waitlist emails and analytics data

## Comparisons

Honest, non-adversarial deep dives: what you keep, what Alfredo adds, and when to stay put. Each compares Alfredo and one tool across seven capabilities (runs on your servers, deploys, wires each project at birth, one dashboard across projects, sees your business, works without assembly, flat cost for many projects).

- [Alfredo vs Coolify](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-coolify): same deploy problem on your servers, plus wiring and one HQ
- [Alfredo vs Dokploy](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-dokploy): deploys your VPS projects, plus the layer above it
- [Alfredo vs Vercel](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-vercel): self-host both halves instead of renting one
- [Alfredo vs Railway](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-railway): own your hardware and the cross-project view
- [Alfredo vs Supabase](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-supabase): wire and watch all your projects, not one backend
- [Alfredo vs Better-T-Stack](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-better-t-stack): use both — it writes code, Alfredo runs it
- [Alfredo vs Grafana](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-grafana): the assembled view, instead of building it
- [Alfredo vs Datadog](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-datadog): one view for many small projects, instead of renting it
- [Alfredo vs Cloudflare](https://getalfredo.com/compare/alfredo-vs-cloudflare): self-host both halves instead of renting the edge
